summary: Re-review: W1 (422 echo) RESOLVED via encode (HTML-sink + double-decode bypass closed, adversarially tested); W2 (telemetry userId) RESOLVED via authenticated req.user.id threaded to enqueue, no prompt/PII/key co-threaded. No new BLOCK/WARN.

findings.claim: Prior WARN W1 (denylist-strip on 422 echo) is RESOLVED
findings.evidence: Line 136-138 now encodes /[&<>"'`=/\x00-\x1F\x7F]/g to decimal HTML entities. Adversarial spot-test of the exact regex:
findings.fix: Resolved — no action. Lint nits (no-control-regex/no-useless-escape) referred to ernie; not security.

findings.claim: Prior WARN W2 (telemetry userId:null attribution gap) is RESOLVED
findings.evidence: req.user.id (authenticated, set by authenticateJWT — not client-supplied) threaded controller:56 -> callGemini:107 -> generate({userId}):25 -> trackedGeminiCall:9 -> enqueue -> ai_usage_outbox.user_id. Same id already trusted for quota. enqueue payload (ai-usage-queue.service.js:12) persists only user_id/use_case/model_name/model_params(config, NOT contents)/tokens/latency/error — no prompt text, PII, or API key co-threaded.

refers.note: ESLint no-control-regex (\x00,\x1f) and no-useless-escape (\/ in char class) on the W1 encoder regex. Control-char match is intentional/correct (it IS the security control); needs a lint-disable comment or drop the redundant \/. Style/correctness, NOT security.
